 {"id":"bd-5","title":"Phase 5: Gum Integration","description":"**EPIC: Beautiful Terminal UI with Gum**

## Goal
Integrate charmbracelet/gum for beautiful terminal UI while maintaining full functionality when gum is unavailable.

## Rationale
Terminal UX matters. A beautiful, informative display builds user confidence and makes the tool pleasant to use. However, we can't require gum - CI environments and minimal systems won't have it. Every gum feature needs an ANSI fallback.

## Gum Features to Use
- gum style: Boxed banners and styled text
- gum confirm: Yes/No prompts
- gum spin: Spinners during operations
- gum choose: Multi-select menus (future)

## Fallback Strategy
Check GUM_AVAILABLE at startup. If false, all gum_* wrapper functions use ANSI codes and basic read prompts instead. The user experience degrades gracefully but remains functional.

## Success Criteria
- With gum: Beautiful boxed output, spinners, styled prompts
- Without gum: Functional ANSI-colored output, basic prompts
- No crashes or errors in either mode","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:52.202922581-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:52.202922581-05:00","close_reason":"Gum Integration EPIC complete - check_gum() and gum_confirm() implemented with ANSI fallbacks at lines 536-569","labels":["gum","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-5","depends_on_id":"bd-4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.103749202-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-501","title":"Implement check_gum()","description":"**Detect gum availability**

## What
Function to check if charmbracelet/gum is installed and set GUM_AVAILABLE flag.

## Why
Gum provides beautiful UI, but we can't require it. We need graceful degradation.

## Implementation
```bash
check_gum() {
    if command -v gum &>/dev/null; then
        GUM_AVAILABLE=true
    else
        GUM_AVAILABLE=false
    fi
}
```

## When to Check
Called once at script startup in main(), before any UI output.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Correctly detects gum presence
- Sets global flag for other functions
- No error output if gum missing","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:48.939151028-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:48.939151028-05:00","close_reason":"check_gum() implemented at lines 536-540","labels":["gum","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-501","depends_on_id":"bd-5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.888569581-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-501","depends_on_id":"bd-205","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.919379012-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-502","title":"Implement gum_confirm()","description":"**Yes/No confirmation with fallback**

## What
Function for yes/no prompts using gum confirm with fallback to read.

## Why
We need confirmations for destructive operations and optional installations. Must work with or without gum.

## Implementation
```bash
gum_confirm() {
    local prompt="$1"
    local default="${2:-n}"  # Default to No for safety
    
    if ! can_prompt; then
        # Non-interactive: return based on default
        [[ "$default" == "y" ]]
        return
    fi
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        gum confirm "$prompt"
    else
        local response
        local yn="y/N"
        [[ "$default" == "y" ]] && yn="Y/n"
        echo -n "$prompt [$yn]: " >&2
        read -r response
        response="${response:-$default}"
        [[ "${response,,}" == "y" || "${response,,}" == "yes" ]]
    fi
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Uses gum when available
- Falls back to read prompt
- Respects non-interactive mode
- Defaults safely to No","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:59.747638163-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:29:59.747638163-05:00","close_reason":"gum_confirm() already implemented at lines 543-569 with ANSI fallback","labels":["gum","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-502","depends_on_id":"bd-501","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.948098104-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-502","depends_on_id":"bd-207","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:09.977713455-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
-{"id":"bd-503","title":"Implement gum_spin()","description":"**Spinner with fallback**

## What
Function to show a spinner during operations with fallback to simple output.

## Why
Spinners provide feedback during potentially long operations. Without gum, we show static messages.

## Implementation
```bash
gum_spin() {
    local title="$1"
    shift
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" && "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]]; then
        gum spin --spinner dot --title "$title" -- "$@"
    else
        [[ "$QUIET_MODE" != "true" ]] && echo -e "${CYAN}→${NC} $title" >&2
        "$@"
    fi
}
```

## Note on Usage
Not heavily used in v1 since we show per-repo progress. May be useful for operations like 'Checking for updates...'

## What
Function to print the tool banner at startup.

## Why
A nice banner establishes identity and professionalism. Different styles for gum vs ANSI.

## With Gum
```
╭──────────────────────────────────────╮
│  🔄 ru v1.0.0                        │
│  Repo Updater                        │
╰──────────────────────────────────────╯
```

## Without Gum (ANSI)
```
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
  ru v1.0.0 - Repo Updater
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
```

## Implementation
```bash
print_banner() {
    [[ "$QUIET_MODE" == "true" ]] && return
    
    if [[ "$GUM_AVAILABLE" == "true" ]]; then
        gum style --border rounded --padding "0 2" \
            "🔄 ru v$VERSION" "Repo Updater"
    else
        echo -e "${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}" >&2
        echo -e "  ${BOLD}ru${NC} v$VERSION - Repo Updater" >&2
        echo -e "${BOLD}━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━${NC}" >&2
    fi
}
```

 {"id":"bd-505","title":"Test gum available and unavailable modes","description":"**Test both UI modes**

## What
Verify the tool works correctly with and without gum installed.

## Why
Users without gum must have full functionality. We can't assume everyone has it.

## Test Scenarios
1. With gum installed: Full beautiful output
2. Without gum: Functional ANSI fallback
3. With --quiet: Minimal output in both modes
4. Non-interactive: No prompts in either mode

## Test Method
```bash
# Test without gum
PATH_WITHOUT_GUM=$(echo $PATH | tr ':' '\n' | grep -v gum | tr '\n' ':')
PATH="$PATH_WITHOUT_GUM" ru sync --dry-run

# Test with gum
ru sync --dry-run
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- No crashes when gum missing
- All functions work in both modes
- Prompts work or fail gracefully","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["testing","ui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-505","depends_on_id":"bd-502","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.117877643-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-505","depends_on_id":"bd-503","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.14740588-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-505","depends_on_id":"bd-504","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.175920177-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-6","title":"Phase 6: Dependency Management","description":"**EPIC: GitHub CLI Detection & Dependency Management**

## Goal
Implement robust detection and guided installation of dependencies, particularly the GitHub CLI (gh), without ever auto-installing without consent.

## Rationale
The tool needs gh for cloning private repos and API access. But automatically installing system packages is invasive and breaks trust. We detect, inform, and prompt - never auto-install unless explicitly requested.

## Dependency Flow
1. Check if gh is installed (command -v gh)
2. If missing and can_prompt(): offer to install with user confirmation
3. If missing and non-interactive: fail with clear instructions
4. If installed: check if authenticated (gh auth status)
5. If not authed and can_prompt(): offer to run gh auth login
6. If not authed and non-interactive: fail with GH_TOKEN instructions

## Non-Interactive Mode
CI/automation needs `--non-interactive` to never hang on prompts. In this mode, missing deps or auth always fails immediately with actionable error messages.

## Success Criteria
- Interactive mode prompts for missing deps
- Non-interactive mode fails cleanly with instructions
- `ru doctor` reports all dependency status clearly","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:52.086156196-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:31:52.086156196-05:00","close_reason":"All dependency management functions implemented: check_gh_installed, check_gh_auth, ensure_dependencies, detect_os","labels":["deps","gh"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6","depends_on_id":"bd-5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.132125739-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-601","title":"Implement detect_os()","description":"**Detect operating system**

## What
Function to detect if running on macOS or Linux.

## Why
Installation commands differ by OS. We need to know which instructions to give.

## Implementation
```bash
detect_os() {
    case "$(uname -s)" in
        Darwin) echo "macos" ;;
        Linux)  echo "linux" ;;
        *)      echo "unknown" ;;
    esac
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns 'macos' on macOS
- Returns 'linux' on Linux
- Returns 'unknown' for others","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:42.136968332-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:30:42.136968332-05:00","close_reason":"detect_os() implemented at lines 641-649","labels":["deps"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-601","depends_on_id":"bd-6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.206049145-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
@@ -97,7 +97,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-702","title":"Implement normalize_url()","description":"**Normalize URLs for comparison**

## What
Convert any URL format to canonical HTTPS form for comparison.

## Why
We need to compare expected vs actual remote URLs. Different formats of the same repo must match.

## Implementation
```bash
normalize_url() {
    local url="$1"
    # Strip .git suffix
    url="${url%.git}"
    # Convert SSH to HTTPS
    url="${url/git@github.com:/https://github.com/}"
    # Lowercase host (github.com vs GitHub.com)
    echo "$url" | tr '[:upper:]' '[:lower:]'
}
```

## Examples
- `git@github.com:owner/repo.git` -> `https://github.com/owner/repo`
- `https://GitHub.com/Owner/Repo` -> `https://github.com/owner/repo`

## Acceptance Criteria
- SSH URLs converted to HTTPS
- .git suffix stripped
- Lowercase for case-insensitive comparison","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.290241568-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.290241568-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing","urls"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-702","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.590058106-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-703","title":"Implement url_to_local_path()","description":"**Resolve URL to local filesystem path**

## What
Function to determine where a repo should live locally based on layout setting.

## Why
Different layout modes organize repos differently. This function encapsulates that logic.

## Layout Modes
- flat: `$PROJECTS_DIR/repo`
- owner-repo: `$PROJECTS_DIR/owner/repo`
- full: `$PROJECTS_DIR/github.com/owner/repo`

## Implementation
```bash
url_to_local_path() {
    local url="$1"
    local projects_dir="$2"
    local layout="$3"

    local host owner repo
    parse_repo_url "$url" host owner repo || return 1

    case "$layout" in
        flat)
            echo "${projects_dir}/${repo}"
            ;;
        owner-repo)
            echo "${projects_dir}/${owner}/${repo}"
            ;;
        full)
            echo "${projects_dir}/${host}/${owner}/${repo}"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown layout: $layout"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All layout modes work
- Invalid layout returns error
- Path uses resolved components","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.291917064-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.291917064-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. All tests pass.","labels":["parsing","paths"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-703","depends_on_id":"bd-701","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.617877674-05:00","created_by":"daemon"},{"issue_id":"bd-703","depends_on_id":"bd-203","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.647842784-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-704","title":"Implement url_to_clone_target()","description":"**Generate clone target for gh repo clone**

## What
Function to generate the owner/repo string that gh repo clone expects.

## Why
gh repo clone takes 'owner/repo', not full URL. We extract this from parsed URL.

## Implementation
```bash
url_to_clone_target() {
    local url="$1"
    local host owner repo
    parse_repo_url "$url" host owner repo || return 1
    echo "${owner}/${repo}"
}
```

## Note on Enterprise
For GitHub Enterprise, gh uses --hostname. Future enhancement.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns owner/repo format
- Works with all input URL formats","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.293471122-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:24:14.293471122-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all URL parsing functions: parse_repo_url, normalize_url, url_to_local_path, url_to_clone_target. -{"id":"bd-705","title":"Implement sanitize_path_segment()","description":"**Sanitize path components**

## What
Remove or replace unsafe characters from path segments.

## Why
Malicious or unusual repo names could cause filesystem issues. Defense in depth.

## Implementation
```bash
sanitize_path_segment() {
    local segment="$1"
    # Remove leading/trailing whitespace
    segment="${segment#"${segment%%[![:space:]]*}"}"
    segment="${segment%"${segment##*[![:space:]]}"}"
    # Replace potentially problematic characters
    segment="${segment//\//_}"
    segment="${segment//\\/_}"
    segment="${segment//:/_}"
    echo "$segment"
}
```

## Characters to Handle
- Slashes (/ \)
- Colons (problematic on Windows)
- Leading dots (hidden files)
- Control characters

## Acceptance Criteria
- Dangerous characters replaced
- Empty segments rejected
- Normal names pass through unchanged","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","labels":["parsing","safety"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-705","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.707274655-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}

 {"id":"bd-706","title":"Create scripts/test_parsing.sh","description":"**Unit tests for URL parsing**

## What
Test script to verify URL parsing works for all formats.

## Why
Parsing is critical. Bugs here cause repos to be cloned to wrong locations or fail entirely.

## Test Cases
```bash
# HTTPS URLs
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"
assert_parses "https://github.com/owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# SSH URLs
assert_parses "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Shorthand
assert_parses "owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# With host
assert_parses "github.com/owner/repo" "github.com" "owner" "repo"

# Normalization
assert_normalizes "git@github.com:owner/repo.git" "https://github.com/owner/repo"

# Local paths
assert_path "owner/repo" "flat" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/repo"
assert_path "owner/repo" "owner-repo" "/data/projects" "/data/projects/owner/repo"

```

 {"id":"bd-8","title":"Phase 8: Git Operations","description":"**EPIC: Git Clone/Pull Operations with Plumbing**

## Goal
Implement git operations using plumbing commands for reliable status detection, avoiding string parsing of porcelain output.

## Rationale
Parsing 'Already up to date' is fragile - it varies by git version and locale. Git plumbing commands give deterministic, parseable output. We use `git rev-list --left-right --count` for ahead/behind, `git status --porcelain` for dirty detection.

## Key Patterns
- Always use `git -C "$repo_path"` instead of cd
- Capture output with `if output=$(git ... 2>&1); then ... else exit_code=$?; fi`
- Check for divergence by comparing old_head vs new_head after pull
- Support multiple strategies: ff-only (safe default), rebase, merge

## Remote Mismatch Detection
If a local repo exists but origin URL differs from expected, we warn and skip. This prevents accidentally overwriting unrelated repos.

## Success Criteria
- Clone operations work with proper error handling
- Pull operations detect all status conditions (current, updated, diverged, conflict)
- No string parsing of user-facing git messages","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.457035826-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["core","git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8","depends_on_id":"bd-7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:08.189443577-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
 {"id":"bd-801","title":"Implement is_git_repo()","description":"**Check if directory is a git repository**

## What
Function to verify a directory is a valid git repository.

## Why
Before pulling, we need to confirm the directory is actually a git repo, not just a regular directory with the same name.

## Implementation
```bash
is_git_repo() {
    local dir="$1"
    [[ -d "$dir/.git" ]] || git -C "$dir" rev-parse --git-dir &>/dev/null
}
```

## Why Both Checks?
- .git directory check is fast
- git rev-parse handles worktrees and bare repos

## Acceptance Criteria
- Returns 0 for valid git repos
- Returns 1 for non-repos
- Works with standard and non-standard git layouts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:10:05.881422408-05:00","updated_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.425291646-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-03T16:26:52.425291646-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented all git operations: is_git_repo, get_repo_status, get_remote_url, check_remote_mismatch, do_clone, do_pull, do_fetch. All tests pass.","labels":["git"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-801","depends_on_id":"bd-8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-03T16:14:10.854102128-05:00","created_by":"daemon"}]}
